title: `idmap` option on Quadlet `Mount` option is handled incorrectly
body:
### Issue Description

`idmap` option on Quadlet `Mount` option is handled incorrectly, resulting in an invalid `podman run` call in the generated `ExecStart`: The arguments to `idmap` are truncated at the first `=` in the id-mapping. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Add the following option to your `.container` file:

```
Mount=type=bind,source=./src/,destination=/dst/,idmap=uids=12-34-1;gids=56-78-1
```

### Describe the results you received

Output of `/usr/libexec/podman/quadlet -dryrun -user`

```
...
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run ... --mount type=bind,source=/home/podman/.config/containers/systemd/src,destination=/dst/,idmap=uids ...
...
```

Note the missing mapping after `idmap=uids`

### Describe the results you expected

The actual mapping should be included in the generated `ExecStart` and not ignored. 

### podman info output

### Upstream Latest Release

comments.body: I have no experience with the podman code base and not exactly time to delve into it at the moment, so sadly I won't be able to tackle the problem anytime soon.
This issue was already resolved here #20149. It should be part of v4.8.0
